svg.title: E-Waste Management Amendment Rules 2023-2024
svg.desc: Animated Educational Diagram for E-Waste Management Amendment Rules 2023-2024

section.div: The E-Waste (Management) Amendment Rules, 2023, and 2024, have updated the previous rules by introducing new obligations for manufacturers and importers.

Manufacturers must design and develop e-waste in an environmentally friendly manner, reducing the amount of toxic substances and hazardous materials. Importers must ensure that the e-waste is properly labeled and marked with relevant information, including the type of e-waste and any hazardous materials present.
Manufacturer's Design for Environment (DfE) - Manufacturers must design and develop e-waste in an environmentally friendly manner, reducing the amount of toxic substances and hazardous materials.
E-Waste Labeling and Marking - Importers must ensure that the e-waste is properly labeled and marked with relevant information, including the type of e-waste and any hazardous materials present.

section.data-narration: Procedures for E-Waste Collection, Transportation, and Disposal
section.div: Procedures for E-Waste Collection, Transportation, and Disposal
E-Waste Collection Centers - The amended rules require the establishment of e-waste collection centers across the country, where citizens can deposit their e-waste.
Transportation of E-Waste - The rules specify the type of vehicle to be used for transportation of e-waste, ensuring safe handling and disposal.
Consumers are now responsible for disposing of e-waste in an environmentally responsible manner.
Consumers must ensure that the e-waste is disposed of in a way that minimizes harm to the environment and human health.
